reasons: Khufra is a strong direct answer because Kalea's Tidal Strike and enhanced attacks include dash-style interactions.
Khufra can punish Kalea's movement timing when she tries to engage or land into the fight.

proof.summary: Kalea wants to use dash and airborne mobility to start fights, while Khufra can punish or block her movement timing.
worksBestWhen: Khufra saves Bouncing Ball for Kalea's Tidal Strike or landing.
Khufra protects the backline target Kalea wants to grab.
Khufra positions near Kalea's expected entry route.
failureCases: Khufra is displaced before Kalea commits.
Kalea waits for Khufra to use Bouncing Ball elsewhere.

reasons: Kalea's enhanced basic attacks and Tidal Strike can create punish windows for Phoveus when she repeatedly uses movement-based actions near him.
This counter becomes stronger when Kalea must enter forward with dash mobility instead of peeling defensively.

proof.summary: Kalea uses multiple movement-based actions to engage and reposition, while Phoveus can punish repeated blink, dash, or displacement actions near him.
worksBestWhen: Phoveus stays near the target Kalea wants to engage.
Kalea is forced to dash forward rather than peel defensively.
Phoveus has enough follow-up to punish after the trigger.
failureCases: Kalea plays patiently and avoids giving repeated triggers.
Phoveus is kited before he can convert the punish.
Kalea uses her mobility to disengage instead of entering near Phoveus.

reasons: Diggie reduces the value of Kalea's airborne and grab-based teamfight setup.
Kalea wants to displace a target into a bad position, while Diggie can protect allies around that timing.

proof.summary: Kalea wants to start fights with airborne control and Tsunami Slam displacement, while Diggie can protect teammates from the crowd-control payoff.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves ultimate for Kalea's real engage.
Diggie stays near the ally Kalea wants to grab.
Diggie avoids getting displaced before casting protection.
failureCases: Kalea baits Diggie ultimate with a smaller engage.
Diggie is too far from the displaced ally.

reasons: Kalea's passive includes healing herself and nearby low-HP allies.
Baxia reduces healing value, making Kalea's support-fighter sustain less reliable.

proof.summary: Kalea wants to heal through enhanced basic attacks and support nearby allies, while Baxia reduces healing received and weakens that sustain value.
worksBestWhen: Baxia stays close enough to pressure Kalea's healing cycle.
The team focuses targets while heal reduction is active.
Baxia contests the fight before Kalea resets through Water Zones.
failureCases: Kalea heals from safe angles behind frontline.
Baxia cannot keep up with Kalea's mobility.
Kalea disengages before Baxia can keep anti-heal pressure active.

reasons: Valentina can copy Kalea's Tsunami Slam and turn Kalea's own displacement threat into a counter-engage tool.
This is useful because Kalea's ultimate has strong fight-starting value through grab, displacement, airborne control, and crowd-control immunity during the cast.

proof.summary: Kalea wants to create teamfight value with Tsunami Slam displacement, while Valentina can copy that ultimate and threaten a similar grab or counter-engage against Kalea's team.
worksBestWhen: Valentina copies Kalea before the decisive teamfight.
Valentina has a safe angle to cast the copied Tsunami Slam.
Valentina's team can follow up after the copied displacement.
failureCases: Valentina copies Kalea but cannot reach a valuable target.
Kalea's team forces Valentina to use the copied ultimate defensively.
Valentina is caught before casting the copied ultimate.
